title: A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England
summary: The checked-in CME00131 XML is a University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding of a discontinuous selection from James Raine Jr.'s 1890 Surtees Society volume, not a transcription of one manuscript and not the complete printed volume. It contains the edition title page and printed pp. 1–29, 35–52, and 58–63. Thus it contains sections I and II, only pp. 22–29 of section III, section IV, and section VI; it omits section III's pp. 30–34, all of section V (pp. 53–57), all of section VII, the glossary appendix, the index, and the other preliminaries, paratext, and apparatus. Section IV's text on printed pp. 51–52 is present even though the XML has no PB milestones for 51 or 52. The selected texts derive from several York civic record groups, Selby manorial court rolls, and a New Malton customs roll reported in 1890; their manuscript, edition, encoding, artifact, and modern scan layers remain distinct. A complete Internet Archive access PDF and its complete supporting derivatives are cached, as are complete Hull History Centre catalog PDFs used to assess the Selby witness. No public complete manuscript facsimile was found on 2026-07-13, and the current location of the New Malton roll remains unresolved.
review_notes: The encoded scope is stated by printed pages, not merely as sections I–IV and VI: title page plus pp. 1–29, 35–52, and 58–63, with the explicit exclusions recorded in the summary and selection practice.

The XML's PB sequence cannot alone delimit the included text. Printed pp. 51–52 of section IV are textually present after PB 50, although PB 51 and PB 52 are absent; phrase and content comparison establishes that boundary.

The current York references Y/COU/1/4/1, Y/COU/1/4/2, and Y/COU/1/1 are supported at volume or series level. Raine's legacy A/Y, B/Y, and Roman-numbered House Book locators have not been silently converted into current component shelfmarks.

The Selby relation is assigned to U DDLO/21 at aggregate level, with dated child candidates recorded as candidates rather than exact excerpt matches. The distinct U DWE catalog was cached and consulted as a rejected alternative, not conflated with the source series.

Complete-facsimile negatives refer only to public web access checked on 2026-07-13. Explore York and Hull History Centre provide onsite consultation and mediated copying routes, but reproduction and publication conditions remain separate from access.
The New Malton customs witness is modeled from Raine's 1890 statement. No current repository, shelfmark, survival confirmation, or public surrogate has been established.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00131
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 125,838 bytes at the recorded CME submodule commit.
It contains 2 DIV1, 5 DIV2, 50 DIV3, 52 PB, 157 P, 33 NOTE, and one TABLE element. The 5 DIV2 and 50 DIV3 counts describe the encoding structure, not physical manuscript units.

The TEMPHEAD records a later review dated 2016-08-30, including removal of a fragment and checks of page-break completeness; it does not establish the original keying date or prove that every printed page milestone is present.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00131
entities.description: A public structured digital text encoding a discontinuous selection from Raine's 1890 printed edition.

date_statements.label: documented XML review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD date documents later review and correction, not initial encoding or publication.

notes: The exact encoded carrier selection is the title page plus printed pp. 1–29, 35–52, and 58–63.
It omits section III pp. 30–34, section V pp. 53–57, all of section VII, the glossary appendix, the index, and other preliminaries, paratext, and apparatus.
The XML declares TEXT/@LANG as enm; the selection also contains Latin headings or formulae and modern English editorial matter.

The PB sequence has two unnumbered milestones, numbered 2–29, 35–50, and 58–63. Textual comparison shows that section IV's printed pp. 51–52 follow PB 50 and are present without corresponding milestones.

entities.label: James Raine Jr.'s 1890 A Volume of English Miscellanies
entities.description: A seven-section documentary miscellany with a glossary appendix and index, compiled and edited from northern English civic, manorial, and literary sources.

bibliographic.citation: James Raine Jr., ed., A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England, Publications of the Surtees Society 85, for the year 1888 (Durham: published for the Society by Andrews & Co. and others, 1890), ix, 100 pp.
bibliographic.title: A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England
bibliographic.series: Publications of the Surtees Society, volume LXXXV

notes: The series leaf says the volume is for 1888, but the title page gives 1890 and the council order is dated 3 December 1889.

The title page does not name an editor. The council order assigns preparation to the Society's Secretary, the preface's autobiographical statement identifies its writer as James Raine Jr., and the Folger catalog explicitly credits James Raine as editor.

The complete volume has seven numbered sections: I pp. 1–10, II pp. 11–22, III pp. 22–34, IV pp. 35–52, V pp. 53–57, VI pp. 58–63, VII pp. 64–75, followed by appendix/glossary pp. 77–93 and index pp. 94–100.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Raine's 1890 volume
entities.description: A complete public access scan of the bound printed carrier contributed by University of California Libraries and digitized by Internet Archive; it is not a facsimile of the medieval manuscript witnesses.
entities.reproduction_format: 120-page color Text PDF derived from a 126-leaf capture sequence; six capture leaves are excluded from access formats
notes: The cached PDF is the provider's complete public access PDF, not a crop to the CME selection.
Internet Archive BookReader leaf n9 is the edition title page; n17–n45 are printed pp. 1–29, n51–n68 are pp. 35–52, and n74–n79 are pp. 58–63.
Intervening leaves n46–n50 (pp. 30–34) and n69–n73 (pp. 53–57), plus the rest of the carrier, are intentionally outside the encoded work portion but remain in the cached object.

entities.label: York Memorandum Book A, Explore York Y/COU/1/4/1
entities.description: A York civic memorandum book used by Raine for the adulteration case and for selected searchers' verdicts and parentage certificates.

date_statements.label: compilation began
date_statements.notes: This date concerns the volume's compilation beginning, not the dates of every record copied into it.

holding.notes: The current volume-level reference is supported. Raine's internal legacy locators require a separate crosswalk.

notes: Raine cites A.Y. 255 for section I and A/Y legacy locators 173, 177b, 178a, 178b, 181a, 182b, 184b, 189a, 192b, 192–3, 198, 326a, 326b, 327b, 343, 351a, 352, and 354a elsewhere in the encoded selection.

The Middle English Grammar Corpus catalog describes the adulteration text as York Memorandum Book A/Y 255, fols. 264v.36–267v.6. Whether 255 is a legacy page or other reference rather than modern foliation remains unresolved.

entities.label: York Memorandum Book B, Explore York Y/COU/1/4/2
entities.description: A York civic memorandum book used by Raine for selected searchers' verdicts and parentage certificates.

date_statements.label: compilation began
date_statements.notes: This date concerns the volume's compilation beginning, not the dates of every record copied into it.

holding.notes: The current volume-level reference is supported; the legacy locators have not been mapped to modern foliation.

notes: Raine's encoded selections cite B/Y legacy locators 87, 145, and 331a. The wording 'Enrolment Book' is treated as a legacy designation for B/Y, not as a separate witness.

entities.label: York Corporation House Books, Explore York Y/COU/1/1
entities.description: The aggregate series of York Corporation House Books from which Raine selected searchers' verdicts and parentage certificates.

holding.notes: Only the current aggregate series is asserted. Raine's Roman-numbered volumes do not support a direct numeric mapping to current child references.

notes: Raine's encoded selections cite House Book I.21 and VIII.121 in section II and I.62, I.76, II.25, VI.41, VI.48, VI.49, VIII.12, VIII.14a, and VIII.27 in section IV.

entities.label: Manor of Selby cum Membris court rolls, Hull History Centre U DDLO/21
alternate_labels: Court-rolls of the Abbots of Selby
entities.description: The current aggregate series of Selby manor court rolls within the Papers of the Earls of Londesborough, supported as the present archival context for Raine's section III jury presentments.

historical_designations: Court-rolls of the Abbots of Selby
originals in the possession of the Earl of Londesborough
holding.notes: The series-level relation is supported by Raine's provenance statement and the current Earls of Londesborough collection. Exact component matching remains open.

notes: Date-compatible candidate children are U DDLO/21/27 (1471–72), /28 (1472–73), /29 (1473–74), /30 (1475–76), /32 (1477), /35 (1479), and /40 (1483–85). They are candidates, not asserted item-level matches.
The eight encoded DIV3 units end on printed p. 29; Raine's section III continues through p. 34 outside the CME selection.
The edition's displayed dates include an internally inconsistent 'June, 14 Edw. IV. 1472,' so date matching alone cannot establish the component crosswalk.

entities.label: New Malton customs original roll reported by Raine in 1890
alternate_labels: Original roll of the customs and liberties of the burgesses of New Malton
entities.description: A fifteenth-century customs roll said by Raine to have been in the possession of Earl Fitzwilliam in 1890; its current repository, shelfmark, survival, and extent have not been established.

identifiers.value: original roll in the possession of Earl Fitzwilliam

notes: Raine provides no shelfmark, physical description, or statement proving whether section VI transcribes the whole roll.

North Yorkshire Archives' Fitzwilliam Family of Malton archive ZPB begins in 1593, so that collection description does not identify this fifteenth-century roll and is not treated as its current holding.

entities.label: Jinming Yi's 2022 study of York civic administrative literacy
entities.description: A complete University of York doctoral thesis used as a scholarly description and modern-reference concordance for York civic books, explicitly mapping Y/COU/1/4/1 to York Memorandum Book A/Y and Y/COU/1/4/2 to York Memorandum Book B/Y.

bibliographic.citation: Jinming Yi, A Study of York's Civic Administrative Literacy: Writing, Records and Archives, 1272–1377 (PhD thesis, University of York, 2022).
bibliographic.title: A Study of York's Civic Administrative Literacy: Writing, Records and Archives, 1272–1377

notes: The thesis is supporting scholarship, not a facsimile of either memorandum book. Its complete public PDF is cached so the cited concordance remains locally inspectable.

entities.label: Hull History Centre U DWE catalog, consulted comparison
entities.description: A complete public catalog PDF for a distinct collection containing Selby Abbey material and earlier court rolls. It was consulted to test, and reject, conflation with the date-compatible U DDLO/21 series.
notes: U DWE has a different custodial history and its described court rolls are chiefly 1377–1403, so it is not asserted as Raine's section III source. The cached catalog documents this negative source evaluation.

agents.name: James Raine Jr.
notes: Editor, compiler, transcriber, commentator, and Surtees Society Secretary; distinct from his father, James Raine (1791–1858).

access.access_method: Use the Explore York CalmView record for the current volume reference.
Consult the original at Explore York Archives by advance booking and subject to repository handling conditions.

Request a complete-object reproduction: covers or boards, spine and edges if possible, pastedowns, flyleaves and blanks, every folio recto and verso, inserts, attachments, endorsements, and damaged surfaces; identify Raine's selected passages separately rather than ordering only excerpts.
No complete public online manuscript surrogate was located; use onsite or mediated copying routes.
Consult by advance booking under Explore York's reading-room conditions.
Request every surface of the complete bound volume and all attachments or inserts; map Raine's B/Y passages separately.
No complete public online manuscript surrogate was located.
After resolving the legacy-to-current component crosswalk, consult the identified volumes by advance booking.

For each identified House Book, request the complete physical object, including binding and all rectos, versos, blanks, inserts, attachments, and damaged surfaces; record selected passage locations separately.
No complete public online surrogate for all source volumes was located.
Use the public U DDLO/21 hierarchy and the complete collection catalog PDF.

This is a complete catalog object, not a manuscript facsimile.

rights.component: checked-in XML encoding and encoded text
rights.basis: The source artifact reproduces U-M's CME00131 encoding, whose item and collection rights statements identify the materials as public domain in the United States.

rights.component: U-M digital encoding and encoded edition text
rights.basis: The U-M item and collection pages state that the materials are in the public domain.
The edition was published in 1890; U-M marks the encoded materials public domain and Wikimedia Commons marks the corresponding Internet Archive volume public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan images and derived access files
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata exposes no rights or license field for the item; Wikimedia Commons identifies its derived copy as public domain in the United States. This supports no-known-copyright rather than an inferred provider license for the exact cached bytes.
notes: The Wikimedia Commons PDF is a downstream derivative and differs byte-for-byte from the cached Internet Archive PDF; its rights statement is therefore evidence about the underlying work, not an exact-file license declaration by Internet Archive.

rights.component: Jinming Yi's thesis text and deposited PDF
rights.basis: The White Rose eTheses landing page identifies the thesis as licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International.

rights.component: new repository-supplied or researcher-made manuscript images
rights.basis: Explore York's fees and charges distinguish research copying from publication and require permission for publication uses.

rights.component: new repository-supplied or researcher-made manuscript images
rights.basis: Explore York's copying and publication conditions apply independently of the age of the manuscript text.

rights.component: new repository-supplied or researcher-made manuscript images
rights.basis: Explore York's copying and publication conditions apply independently of the age of the manuscript text.
Hull History Centre offers research-quality copying for personal research and treats publication permission and fees separately.
No current holding repository or image provider has been identified, so image rights and contractual conditions cannot be assessed.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Raine's complete seven-section volume with preliminaries, glossary appendix, and index
editorial_practices.representation: Edition title page and printed pp. 1–29, 35–52, and 58–63
editorial_practices.description: CME00131 makes a discontinuous page-level selection. It includes sections I and II, only the first eight editorial units of section III through p. 29, section IV including the text of pp. 51–52 despite absent PB milestones, and section VI. It excludes section III pp. 30–34, section V pp. 53–57, all of section VII, the glossary appendix, the index, and the other preliminaries, paratext, and apparatus.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole digital encoding relative to the 1890 carrier
notes: The PB sequence alone undercounts included printed surfaces because pp. 51–52 have no PB milestones; content comparison establishes their inclusion.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Separate York civic books, Selby court rolls, and a New Malton customs roll
editorial_practices.representation: Thematically grouped documentary sections with editorial headings, dates, source locators, and notes
editorial_practices.description: Raine selected and arranged records from multiple physical and institutional sources into thematic sections. The resulting edition is a documentary miscellany, not a diplomatic edition of one witness.
editorial_practices.scope: Sections I–VI of the carrier, with CME00131 encoding only the page portions stated separately

open_questions.question: How do Raine's A/Y, B/Y, and Roman-numbered House Book locators map to current foliation and child shelfmarks, including the relationship between A.Y. 255 and fols. 264v.36–267v.6?
next_steps: Request or inspect Explore York's legacy finding aids and concordances.
Compare every Raine incipit and explicit legacy locator against the current volumes rather than assuming numeric continuity.
Record the crosswalk at passage level while preserving both locator systems.

open_questions.question: Which U DDLO/21 court-roll components and membranes contain the eight CME-encoded section III presentments, and how should their edition dates be reconciled?
next_steps: Ask Hull History Centre for item-level descriptions, endorsements, or legacy concordances for U DDLO/21/27–30, /32, /35, and /40.
Compare incipits, jury place names, regnal dates, and membrane sequences against complete roll images.
Do not assign an exact child solely from Raine's sometimes inconsistent marginal dates.

open_questions.question: Where is the fifteenth-century New Malton customs roll reported in Earl Fitzwilliam's possession in 1890, and does it survive under a current shelfmark?
next_steps: Contact North Yorkshire Archives, the Fitzwilliam (Malton) Estate, and repositories holding Wentworth-Fitzwilliam papers with Raine's exact title, incipit, and provenance statement.
Search estate transfer, sale, and deposit records rather than assuming the post-1593 ZPB collection contains the roll.
If located, request a complete-object surrogate and establish whether Raine printed the whole roll or a selection.

open_questions.question: Why does CME00131 stop section III at printed p. 29 and omit section V, while retaining section IV pp. 51–52 without PB milestones despite the 2016 review note saying PB completeness was checked?
next_steps: Inspect surviving keying specifications, proofsheets, production logs, or U-M project documentation.
Treat the current text and phrase boundaries as evidence of inclusion and omission; do not manufacture PB 51 or PB 52 or restore omitted text without provenance.
Record any future repair as a new encoding intervention rather than retroactively changing source lineage.
